response.justification: The role demands a deep, current C++ practitioner with 8–12 years of hands-on systems-level development, yet Bogdan's C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his skills list. His career trajectory has moved firmly toward TypeScript/Node.js web stacks and DLang, with no evidence of STL proficiency, multi-threaded C++ systems, MQTT, industrial protocols, or IEC 62443 secure-development practices — all of which are core, not advantageous, requirements for this position.
strengths: Genuine event-driven and distributed-systems experience (Dunnhumby micro-services at millions of req/s, GISCollective geo-data platform) maps loosely to message-broker and scalable-architecture themes in the JD
Strong agile craft (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pair programming) aligns with the role's agile-collaboration and quality-mindset requirements
Breadth across embedded, mobile, backend, and IoT-adjacent domains (NI LabWindows, Apple TV apps, GIS platform) shows adaptability and systems thinking
concerns: No current or substantive C++ expertise — the single explicit requirement of the role — making the candidate fundamentally unqualified for the position as written
Zero evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge, SDL processes, or IEC 62443 compliance, which are non-negotiable for this sector
No industrial automation, MQTT, OPC UA, or any relevant protocol experience; the candidate's domain is entirely web/cloud SaaS, not OT/ICS environments
